    _frozen

    Protected_frozen

    _frozen: boolean

    Whether the node is frozen.

    Remarks

    As nodes are initialized without a parent and possibly attached to a parent after construction, this makes hierarchies of nodes mutable. That is, a node without a parent node may be assigned one by being passed as a child to a parent node. When this happens, a node becomes frozen. Nodes can also become frozen before being assigned a parent by using the Node#freeze() method.

    Inherited from BaseNode._frozen

    • Defined in alfa-tree/dist/tree.d.ts:44

        • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<

        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns string

      Overrides BaseNode._internalPath

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/attribute.ts:118

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns Sequence<Node>

    closest

    closest

    • closest<T extends Node>(
          refinement: Refinement<Node, T>,
          options?: {
              add(
                  ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
              ): any;
              equals(value: any): boolean;
              equals(value: unknown): value is any;
              has(flag: 0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested"): boolean;
              is(
                  ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
              ): boolean;
              isSet: (
                  flag: 0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested",
              ) => boolean;
              kind: "DOM traversal";
              remove(
                  ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
              ): any;
              set: (
                  ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
              ) => any;

                  ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                  boolean,
              >;

              ) => any;
              value: number;

                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,

          Test whether a given flag is present (or set) in the set of flags

        • remove: function
          • remove(
                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
            ): any

            Parameters

            • ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:342
        • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:355
        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns Option<T>

      Inherited from BaseNode.closest

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:348

        • isSet: (flag: 0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested") => boolean

          Test whether a given flag is present (or set) in the set of flags

        • Readonlykind: "DOM traversal"
        • remove: function
          • remove(
                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
            ): any

            Removes a list of flags from the set, and return a new one.

            Parameters

            • ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">

            Returns any

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:342
        • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:355
        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns Option<Node>

      Inherited from BaseNode.first

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:343

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return.     freeze

    freeze

    • freeze(): this

      Freeze the node. This prevents further expansion of the node hierarchy, meaning that the node can no longer be passed as a child to a parent node.

      Returns this

      Inherited from BaseNode.freeze

      • Defined in alfa-tree/dist/tree.d.ts:59

          Test whether a given flag is present (or set) in the set of flags

        • remove: function
          • remove(
                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,

        • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:355
        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns Sequence<Node>

      Inherited from BaseNode.inclusiveSiblings

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:339

              ) => any;

                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,

          Test whether a given flag is present (or set) in the set of flags

        • Readonlykind: "DOM traversal"
        • remove: function
          • remove(
                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
            ): any

            Removes a list of flags from the set, and return a new one.

            • ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:342
        • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:355
        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns boolean

      Inherited from BaseNode.isDescendantOf

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:330

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns boolean

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns boolean

      Inherited from BaseNode.isInclusiveSiblingOf

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:340

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return.         • isSet: (flag: 0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested") => boolean

          Test whether a given flag is present (or set) in the set of flags

        • Readonlykind: "DOM traversal"
        • remove: function
          • remove(
                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
            ): any

            Removes a list of flags from the set, and return a new one.

            Parameters

            • ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">

            Returns any

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:342
        • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:355
        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns Option<Node>

      Inherited from BaseNode.last

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:344

          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns Option<Node>

      Private Remarks

      The type assertions to Node in here are technically incorrect. BaseNode could be extended to another class which is then not added to the Node union, in which case the assertions would be wrong. This scenario is however quite unlikely given that BaseNode is not exported out of this package. So it is only a matter of us not doing so, which sound OK enough. It is not possible at this level to do any sort of runtime type checking without creating circular dependencies, and the parent method here has to extends the one in alfa-tree/Node.

      Inherited from BaseNode.parent

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:197

      Get an XPath that uniquely identifies the node across descendants of its root.

      Parameters

      • options: {
            add(
                ...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">,
            ): any;
            equals(value: any): boolean;
            equals(value: unknown): value is any;

        • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:355
        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns string

      Inherited from BaseNode.path

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:256

However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return.         • set: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Adds a list of flags to the set (aka sets the flags), and return a new one.

        • toJSON: function
          • toJSON(): JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            Returns JSON<"DOM traversal"> & KeyedByArray<
                ["composed", "flattened", "nested"],
                boolean,
            >

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:355
        • toString: function
          • toString(): string

            Returns string

            • Defined in alfa-flags/dist/flags.d.ts:354
        • unset: (...flags: Array<0 | 2 | 1 | 4 | "composed" | "flattened" | "nested">) => any

          Removes a list of flags to the set (aka unsets the flags), and return a new one.

        • Readonlyvalue: number

          A compact representation of the set of flags as a number

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return. However, given the dynamic nature of it (with the named fields), this gets very tricky. Given that this field is just a number, it is easier to make it public and forgo the getter.

      Returns Sequence<Node>

      Inherited from BaseNode.siblings

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:337

    tabOrder

    tabOrder

    • tabOrder(this: Node): Sequence<Element<string>>

      Construct a sequence of descendants of this node sorted by tab index. Only nodes with a non-negative tab index are included in the sequence.

      https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#tabindex-value

      Parameters

      • this: Node

      Returns Sequence<Element<string>>

      Inherited from BaseNode.tabOrder

      • Defined in alfa-dom/src/node/node.ts:59

          Private Remarks

          We usually have protected fields and getters for these cases. However, this makes TS unhappy about the class factory named. https://github.com/microsoft/TypeScript/issues/17744 We could (should?) craft the correct type of the class for the factory to return.
